answers.What kind of connection are you looking: Honestly just people to hang out with regularly. I moved to Toronto eight months ago and I still don't really have a friend group here, which is kind of embarrassing to admit but it is what it is.
answers.What has been missing socially in your l: Consistency I guess. I'll meet someone at an event and we'll say we should hang out and then nothing happens. I want people I actually see more than once.
answers.What kind of group would genuinely feel: Small. Like 4-6 people max. I get kind of overwhelmed in bigger crowds and I don't end up talking to anyone real anyway.
answers.What pace of connection feels comfortabl: Slow is fine. I don't need to be best friends immediately, I just want things to keep moving forward and not fizzle out after the first meetup.
answers.In a new group, what helps you feel comf: When there's something to do or talk about. I find it hard when it's just standing around making small talk with no structure. An activity or a topic helps a lot.
answers.What role do you naturally play in a gro: I ask a lot of questions. I'm more of a listener than a talker but once I get comfortable I can get pretty chatty. Takes me a bit though.
answers.What kinds of activities do you actually: Board games, trying new restaurants, walking around neighbourhoods. I'm open to most things as long as it's not a packed bar where you can't hear anyone.
answers.What times / days are generally easiest: Weekends are easiest for me. Weekday evenings I'm usually pretty drained from work and not at my best socially.

answers.What kind of connection are you looking: Something real, I think. I have plenty of acquaintances and work colleagues but actual meaningful friendship has kind of slipped away since my breakup earlier this year. I want people who actually give a shit.
answers.What has been missing socially in your l: Depth. I can make conversation no problem but I haven't had a conversation that actually meant something in a while and that's been getting to me more than I expected.
answers.What kind of group would genuinely feel: I'm fine with different sizes honestly but I think smaller groups tend to go deeper faster. I don't need it to be tiny but something where I'm not just another face.
answers.What pace of connection feels comfortabl: I want things to move. Not rushing it but I don't want to meet every two months and never get past surface level. Regular meetups where you actually build something.
answers.In a new group, what helps you feel comf: When people are genuine and not performing. I can tell pretty fast when someone's just being charming versus actually interested and the latter makes all the difference.
answers.What role do you naturally play in a gro: I tend to steer conversations into more interesting territory. People sometimes call me intense but I prefer to think I just don't like talking about the weather.
answers.What kinds of activities do you actually: Hiking, cooking together, pub trivia, going to talks or documentary screenings. Really anything that gives you something to react to together.
answers.What times / days are generally easiest: My schedule is all over the place right now but I can usually make Thursday evenings work and most of Sunday. Saturdays depend on the week.
